rows.notes: Clear centered page title at top of the page, no monetary columns aligned to this line. Ink legible and appears as a structural label.

rows.description: Taken out of of Treasury, & balance off last year
rows.notes: Right-side set of three vertical ruling lines located using lower totals; numbers read as 53 (pounds), 19 (shillings), 2 (pence). A small stray mark '9' appears in the margin to the right of the pence; interpreted as marginal note and not part of currency. Column alignment consistent with other totals.
Single-word name immediately below header. Handwriting slightly ambiguous (could be 'Guinser' or 'Guiser'). No amounts visible in pounds/shillings/pence columns.
Place/name line readable as 'Longwitham' (likely Long Wittenham). No amounts aligned to this line. Vertical spacing and ink make the letters reasonably clear.
Handwriting unclear; read as 'Muirhinock' (several letters faint). No monetary figures present. Chosen reading is the best plausible transcription but uncertain.
Long dashed leader from description to the right-hand monetary columns; clear reading of 29 pounds, 0 shillings, 0 pence. Description reads as 'South savington Anne' (letters legible).
Name looks like 'Cadlington Ric.' or 'Carrington Ric.'; handwriting of first word a little faint. No amounts present.
Single short place/name; letters readable though not crisp. No monetary alignment.
Clear single-word entry 'Morton' with no amounts. Ink and spacing are legible.
Reads as 'Clear Hill' (could be 'Clare Hill'); moderate clarity. No amounts present.
Short word, somewhat ambiguous — read as 'Phorp' though could be 'Pheup' or similar. No amounts.
Looks like 'Cudlington prior' (handwritten 'prior' faint). No monetary figures.
Name clearly reads 'Lucy Eaton' with a leader to monetary columns showing 2 pounds, 0 shillings, 0 pence. Alignment with vertical rules clear.
Phrase 'The quit rent' clearly written; no amount immediately visible (likely a descriptive sub-item).
Single surname 'Warner' legible; no monetary figures.
Multi-word name appears as 'Ralph marshally Capon' (middle word uncertain: 'marshally' read from script). No amounts visible.
Short surname 'Biggs' clearly written; no monetary figure.
Reads as 'Calott' or 'Calot'; handwriting of one letter ambiguous. No amount present.
Appears as 'Cudlington Gully' (first word similar to other 'Cudlington' entries). No amounts.
Short place/name, likely 'Benyton'/'Bonyton' — letters faint and ambiguous. No monetary values.
Very unclear script; read as 'Quentinante' as best fit. No amounts present.
Short name 'Healy' (or 'Hesly'); ink slightly light. No monetary entries.
Clear leader to monetary columns showing 0 pounds, 15 shillings, 0 pence. 'Greenway' legible; alignment consistent with ruling lines.
Short name read as 'Danifor' or 'Danifor' variant; somewhat faint. No amounts.
Surname 'Franklin' clear; no monetary figures aligned.
Single surname 'Mayne' (or 'Myns'); fairly legible. No amounts.
Reads as 'Ladon Hall' or 'Ladden Hall'; some letters ambiguous but meaning clear as a hall name. No amount present.
Short unclear surname 'Satter' — reading uncertain due to light ink. No amounts.
Surname 'Molineaux' reasonably legible (French form). No monetary entries.

rows.description: Mr Pits of Affey for two years Interest
rows.notes: Text reads 'Mr Pitts of Affey for two years Interest' (handwriting: Mr Pits of Affey ...). Monetary columns clearly show 16 pounds, 0 shillings, 0 pence.
Clear entry 'Hart Hall' with leader to the right-hand columns showing 1 pound, 13 shillings, 4 pence. Numbers are crisp and validate (shillings <20, pence <12).
Two names appear on the same line or closely stacked: 'Puitinholt' with parenthetical 'Hopking Napper' — both faint; no amounts visible. Reading is uncertain.
Place name 'Clifton Ferry' legible but lighter than earlier entries; no amount shown.
Looks like 'Benyngton' / 'Bennington' — letters legible enough to be confident. No monetary columns filled.
Reads as 'Brewthorpe' or 'Brewhouse' (script ambiguous). No amounts visible in columns.
Handwritten text appears to be 'Southnowing Const.' or 'Southmoving Const.' — fairly ambiguous. No monetary figures present.

Bottom-most line has a long dashed leader to the right-hand monetary columns displaying 74 pounds, 18 shillings, 4 pence — clear final total. Description reads as 'Cudlington Augm. Rd.' (abbreviated), legible enough to be confident that this is a section total.
